An individual in adult court is guilty of an offense if the individual engages in any combination of three or more sexual acts or sexual contacts with a minor under the age of fifteen years during a period of three or more months. proposals.source_quote: The Court cannot find by clear and convincing evidence that the conditions and causes of deprivation are likely to continue, or will not be remedied as required by N.D.C.C. 27-20-44(1)(c)(1).
proposals.source: PDF OCR ingestion (pdftotext); verified in rendered page image and cross-referenced against correct citations elsewhere in opinion
proposals.evidence: The lowercase letter 'l' (U+006C) appears where a digit '1' should be in the statutory citation. This is an OCR error from PDF ingestion. The correct form '27-20-44(1)(c)' appears twice elsewhere in the opinion (¶14 and ¶16 describing the same statute), proving the intended citation format. The structure 27-20-44(1)(c)(1) matches North Dakota statutory citation conventions for N.D.C.C. § 27-20-44, subdivision (1), paragraph (c), clause (1).
proposals.note: This is a clear OCR defect where pdftotext rendered the numeral '1' as the letter 'l'. The error appears in a direct quotation from the juvenile court's written order, but the digit error is an ingestion artifact, not the court's own text. The citation is to N.D.C.C. § 27-20-44(1)(c)(1), not (l)(c)(1).

coverage_note: Complete verification performed: (1) Quote character normalization from typographic to straight quotes verified as intentional per policy (Rule 9); 68 typographic quotes in PDF correctly normalized to straight quotes in DB. (2) Whitespace/layout differences between PDF and DB are expected formatting artifacts and not ingestion errors. (3) All statute citations scanned; only one OCR error identified and proposed for fix. (4) Word-level comparison shows identical content after accounting for PDF line-wrapping and page numbering artifacts. (5) No footnote anomalies, no markup contamination, no missing text, no citation fragmentation issues detected."
